decisions.title: Migrating to TypeScript
decisions.summary: Decided to migrate the entire codebase from JavaScript to TypeScript to improve type safety, developer experience, and maintainability.

decisions.title: Migrating to TypeScript
decisions.summary: Decided to migrate the entire codebase from JavaScript to TypeScript to improve type safety, developer experience, and maintainability.

decisions.title: Migrating to TypeScript
decisions.summary: Decided to migrate the entire codebase from JavaScript to TypeScript to improve type safety, developer experience, and maintainability.

decisions.title: API Server Memory Limits
decisions.summary: The API server is capped at 2GB RAM per instance.

decisions.title: Session Management (Redis)
decisions.summary: All user sessions and distributed locks are managed via Redis.

decisions.title: Database Connection Pooling
decisions.summary: Maximum concurrent connections are limited to 20.

rules.rule_text: The application must fully support Hebrew and Arabic interfaces.
rules.scope: All applications using Hebrew and Arabic languages.

rules.rule_text: Use the HTML attribute `dir="rtl"` on the root element when the language is set to Hebrew/Arabic.
rules.scope: All applications using Hebrew and Arabic languages.

rules.rule_text: Avoid using `ml-` (margin-left) or `pr-` (padding-right). Instead, use logical properties like `ms-` (margin-start) and `pe-` (padding-end) to ensure layout flips correctly between LTR and RTL.
rules.scope: All applications using Hebrew and Arabic languages.

rules.rule_text: Text alignment should default to `text-start`.
rules.scope: All applications using Hebrew and Arabic languages.

rules.rule_text: Ensure all text passes WCAG AA contrast ratios against the primary Dark Blue (#001f3f) background.
All interactive elements (buttons, links, inputs) must have a visible `:focus` state.
Every icon-only button must include an `aria-label` describing its function.
Use `<main>`, `<nav>`, and `<section>` tags instead of generic `<div>` wrappers where possible.
Write clean, modular code following DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principles.
Use TypeScript for all new components to ensure type safety.
Document complex functions with JSDoc comments.
Use Tailwind CSS only for all styling. No external CSS files or CSS modules.
All table names should be lowercase and plural.
Always include `created_at` and `updated_at` timestamps for every table.
The API server is capped at 2GB RAM per instance.
Sessions are set to expire after 24 hours of inactivity.
Maximum concurrent connections are limited to 20.
Always use the redis-client wrapper to ensure atomic operations during session updates.
Always release connections in a finally block.
The application must fully support Hebrew and Arabic interfaces.
Use the HTML attribute `dir="rtl"` on the root element when the language is set to Hebrew/Arabic.
Avoid using `ml-` (margin-left) or `pr-` (padding-right). Instead, use logical properties like `ms-` (margin-start) and `pe-` (padding-end) to ensure layout flips correctly between LTR and RTL.
Text alignment should default to `text-start`.

Always include `created_at` and `updated_at` timestamps for every table.
The API server is capped at 2GB RAM per instance.
Sessions are set to expire after 24 hours of inactivity.

Maximum concurrent connections are limited to 20.
Use the pg-pool library to manage recycling of idle connections.
Always release connections in a finally block.
The application must fully support Hebrew and Arabic interfaces.
Use the HTML attribute `dir="rtl"` on the root element when the language is set to Hebrew/Arabic.
Avoid using `ml-` (margin-left) or `pr-` (padding-right). Instead, use logical properties like `ms-` (margin-start) and `pe-` (padding-end) to ensure layout flips correctly between LTR and RTL.
Text alignment should default to `text-start`.
Ensure all text passes WCAG AA contrast ratios against the primary Dark Blue (#001f3f) background.
All interactive elements (buttons, links, inputs) must have a visible `:focus` state.
Every icon-only button must include an `aria-label` describing its function.
Use `<main>`, `<nav>`, and `<section>` tags instead of generic `<div>` wrappers where possible.
Write clean, modular code following DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principles.
Use TypeScript for all new components to ensure type safety.
Document complex functions with JSDoc comments.
Use Tailwind CSS only for all styling. No external CSS files or CSS modules.
All table names should be lowercase and plural.
Always include `created_at` and `updated_at` timestamps for every table.
Avoid large in-memory data processing. Use Node.js streams for handling large JSON payloads or file exports.
Ensure no global variables are used to store temporary request data to prevent memory leaks.
Sessions are set to expire after 24 hours of inactivity.
Always use the redis-client wrapper to ensure atomic operations during session updates. Do not fall back to local memory for session storage.
Use the pg-pool library to manage recycling of idle connections. Always release connections in a finally block.

warnings.message: Do NOT modify the Auth Middleware (src/middleware/auth.ts) under any circumstances without explicit, documented approval from the Lead Developer. Any changes to the JWT verification logic or role-based access control (RBAC) could create severe security vulnerabilities.
